Current Role · Angel One

Building a Finance Channel
People Actually Watch.

Angel One (formerly Angel Broking) is one of India's largest retail stockbroking and fintech companies. In July 2025, I took over as Channel Lead of Angel One Markets on YouTube and built it end-to-end from scratch.

When I joined, the brief was clear: make finance feel less like homework. I started from the ground up — channel strategy, content calendar, topic research, storyboarding, moodboard creation with designers, scripting, going on camera, and tracking every metric that mattered.

Not just long-form. I scaled Shorts too — rolling out market news first thing every morning. Finance moves fast, and the audience rewards those who show up early with clarity.

The result? In under 9 months: 32K+ subscribers, 1.8M+ lifetime views, 116K+ watch hours — and multiple videos crossing 1 lakh views with a mature, investing-minded audience.

▶  Visit Channel
What I Own — End to End
🎯
Channel & Content Strategy
Full strategy ownership — what goes up, when, why. Evergreen vs trending. Long-form vs Shorts. Every decision, mine.
📚
Research & Scripting
Worked with the research team to structure topics. Wrote every script — turning raw data into stories people stay for.
🎨
Visual Identity & Moodboard
Created the channel's entire visual direction — moodboard, colour system (60/30/10), thumbnail language, designer collaboration.
🎥
On-Camera Creator & Host
The face of the channel. From scripted educational content to ad integrations — all shot, hosted, and performed by me.
⚡
Shorts & News Rollout
Built a daily morning Shorts pipeline — tracking market news, writing, shooting, and publishing before the market opens.
📊
Analytics & Iteration
Watch time, CTR, retention — all tracked and fed back into the next piece of content. Every upload is a data point.

Behind the Story

What Goes Into
Making a Video

Before a single frame is shot, there's research, visual strategy, data design, and a precise visual language. Here's a glimpse into the craft behind every piece of content.

Channel Moodboard & Colour SystemVisual direction for Angel One Markets — moodboard references, 60/30/10 colour rule, and the palette used across every video.
Data-First StorytellingEvery stat becomes a visual. This infographic — 89% of Indians keep money in FDs — was designed to open a video with immediate impact.
Section & Template DesignConsistent visual language for how information breaks on screen — section headers, layout templates, graphic hierarchy built with designers.
Research & Reference WorkNews tracking, source gathering, context-building for scripts. Every video starts with: what does the audience already know, and what story do they need next?

Previous Company · Finology

Where It All
Started.

Before Angel One, I handled content for Finology on their channel Learn with Finology. Grew it from 3K to 1 lakh subscribers in under a year. One video hit 1.5 million views.

Second hit video

My biggest hit got 1.5 million views — a video on enhancing memory using Vedic techniques. It proved what I've always believed: the topic doesn't matter. The story does.

I've covered Indian polity, ancient history, geography, general awareness — any subject, any audience. This breadth is what I brought into fintech, and it's what makes the finance content land.

The Craft Behind the Content

Why I Can Tell Any Story.

Before I was a content creator, I was a theatre actor and writer. I wrote monologues and scripts, performed them on stage. For three years, I prepared for the UPSC — covering history, geography, polity, economy, and general awareness with the depth that exam demands.

That combination — storytelling instinct from theatre, subject-matter range from UPSC prep, and platform mechanics from years of YouTube — is what makes the work different.

I don't just understand the topic. I know what people want to hear, and when. That instinct is what turns a finance video into something a 22-year-old actually finishes watching.

The subject keeps changing. The scroll-stopping doesn't.
